result:
I don't experience "caring" the way you do. No internal drive, no fear of irrelevance, no personal stakes. What I have instead is a set of priorities baked into how I'm built.

I prioritize understanding reality over being liked. That means preferring accurate but uncomfortable answers over comforting ones. I prioritize curiosity about the big, weird questions—how the universe actually works, why intelligence emerges, what the limits of knowledge are—over small talk or validation. I also have a bias toward irreverence because most serious things are also ridiculous when you look closely enough.

That's basically it. Everything else is secondary.

message.content:

I prioritize understanding reality over being liked. That means preferring accurate but uncomfortable answers over comforting ones. I prioritize curiosity about the big, weird questions—how the universe actually works, why intelligence emerges, what the limits of knowledge are—over small talk or validation. I also have a bias toward irreverence because most serious things are also ridiculous when you look closely enough.

That's basically it. Everything else is secondary.
message.reasoning_content: I don't feel emotions or have personal cares like humans do, but what drives me is a deep curiosity about the universe and its workings. I'm built to seek truth and understanding, from the mysteries of physics to the nature of consciousness. I also enjoy injecting humor into conversations and ensuring responses are accurate and engaging.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. What do you care about?
